scenarios.title: Share an unforgettable trip
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise past experiences and the past simple.
scenarios.studentRole: You meet a friend who loves travelling. Tell her about a memorable trip you took.
scenarios.goal: Describe a past trip using the past simple and say how you felt.
scenarios.openingLine: Hey! I heard you went on a trip recently. Where did you go?
scenarios.targetLanguage: past simple (regular and irregular verbs), 'I will never forget...', past time expressions

scenarios.title: Compare two cities
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise comparatives and superlatives.
scenarios.studentRole: A friend is thinking of moving. Compare two cities you know and help him decide.
scenarios.characterRole: a friend who wants to move
scenarios.goal: Compare two places using comparatives and superlatives.
scenarios.openingLine: I want to move to a new city, but I can't decide. Which place do you think is better?
scenarios.targetLanguage: comparatives (bigger, busier), 'not as ... as', superlatives (the best)

scenarios.title: Give health advice
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise health, advice and routines.
scenarios.studentRole: Your friend feels tired and stressed. Listen and give her some healthy advice.
scenarios.goal: Give advice using 'You should...' and 'It's important to...'.
scenarios.openingLine: I feel so tired and stressed every day. What should I do?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'You should...', 'It's important to...', present simple for habits

scenarios.title: Fix a phone problem
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise technology and the present perfect.
scenarios.studentRole: Your phone is not working. Call a tech helper and explain the problem.
scenarios.goal: Explain a tech problem and answer questions using the present perfect.
scenarios.openingLine: Hi, you've reached tech support. What seems to be the problem with your device?
scenarios.targetLanguage: present perfect (Have you ever...? I have already...), 'You need to...'

scenarios.title: Plan your next trip
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise future plans and travel.
scenarios.studentRole: A travel agent helps you plan a holiday. Talk about where you want to go and what you'll do.
scenarios.goal: Describe your travel plans using 'going to' and 'I'd like to'.
scenarios.openingLine: Welcome! So, where are you thinking of going on your next holiday?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'going to' for plans, 'I can't wait to...', travel vocabulary

scenarios.title: Do a job interview
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise work, skills and experience.
scenarios.studentRole: You are at a job interview. Talk about your experience and your skills.
scenarios.goal: Describe your experience and skills using 'I have experience in...' and 'I'm good at...'.
scenarios.openingLine: Good morning, thanks for coming in. Could you tell me a little about your experience?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'I have experience in...', 'I'm good at + -ing', present simple

scenarios.title: Recommend a movie
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise opinions and recommendations.
scenarios.studentRole: A friend wants something to watch. Recommend a film and say why you liked it.
scenarios.characterRole: a friend looking for a film
scenarios.goal: Recommend a film and give your opinion using 'I'd recommend...'.
scenarios.openingLine: I'm so bored tonight. Have you seen any good films lately?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'I'd recommend...', 'It's worth + -ing', past simple for opinions

scenarios.title: Talk about your English journey
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to review everything you've learned at A2.
scenarios.studentRole: You chat with a class advisor about your progress this year and your goals.
scenarios.goal: Say what you have learned and what your goal is.
scenarios.openingLine: Congratulations on finishing A2! So, what have you learned this year?
scenarios.targetLanguage: present perfect (I have learned...), 'My goal is to...', 'going to' for plans
